perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years, except for clauses related to intellectual property which will remain in effect for five years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement lasts for two years, but then specifies that intellectual property clauses last for five years. This contradicts the overall two-year term and introduces uncertainty about the actual duration of specific obligations within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights and obligations herein shall bind the parties and their successors and assigns. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes al prior oral or written agreements, commitments and understandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years, except for clauses related to intellectual property which will remain in effect for five years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the agreement lasts for two years, but then adds an exception that intellectual property clauses last for five years. This directly contradicts the initially stated two-year term for the entire agreement, creating an inconsistency as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The contradicted text includes the changed text and highlights the contradictory nature of the statement where it states the agreement is for two years but then for five years for certain clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its Representatives who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its Representatives who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose. However, Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to potential investors without regard to Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause restricts disclosure to representatives with a 'need to know' for the 'Permitted Purpose'. The addition allows disclosure to potential investors without this restriction, directly contradicting the original limitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its Representatives who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose. However, Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to potential investors without regard to Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to potential investors without regard to the 'Permitted Purpose'. This directly contradicts the original clause, which restricts disclosure to representatives with a 'need to know' such information for the 'Permitted Purpose.' The explanation provided states this creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and changed text are in the same location, but the change causes a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (a) was in Recipient's or its Representative's possession prior to disclosure by Discloser hereunder; (b) was generally known, in the trade or business in which it is practiced by Recipient or its Representatives at the time of disclosure to Recipient or its Representatives hereunder, or becomes so generally known after such disclosure, through no act of Recipient or its employees, agents or independent contractors in violation of this Agreement; (c) has come into the possession of Recipient or its Representatives from a third party who is not, to Recipient's knowledge, under any obligation to Discloser to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (d) was developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of and without reference to Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (a) was in Recipient's or its Representative's possession prior to disclosure by Discloser hereunder; (b) was generally known, in the trade or business in which it is practiced by Recipient or its Representatives at the time of disclosure to Recipient or its Representatives hereunder, or becomes so generally known after such disclosure, through no act of Recipient or its employees, agents or independent contractors in violation of this Agreement; (c) has come into the possession of Recipient or its Representatives from a third party who is not, to Recipient's knowledge, under any obligation to Discloser to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (d) was developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of and without reference to Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by adding that 'all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information,' despite the preceding exceptions that define what is *not* considered Confidential Information. This creates ambiguity because, for example, pricing information already in the Recipient's possession (clause a) arguably shouldn't be confidential, but the new clause overrides that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (a) was in Recipient's or its Representative's possession prior to disclosure by Discloser hereunder; (b) was generally known, in the trade or business in which it is practiced by Recipient or its Representatives at the time of disclosure to Recipient or its Representatives hereunder, or becomes so generally known after such disclosure, through no act of Recipient or its employees, agents or independent contractors in violation of this Agreement; (c) has come into the possession of Recipient or its Representatives from a third party who is not, to Recipient's knowledge, under any obligation to Discloser to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (d) was developed by Recipient or its Representatives independently of and without reference to Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information' which directly contradicts the original exceptions to what is not Confidential Information, as stated in the original and contradicted text. For example, information in Recipient's possession is not confidential, but the new clause overrides that. This introduces a clear in-text contradiction because the changed text makes 'all pricing information' confidential, regardless of whether it falls under the exceptions listed in the first part of the section. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations regarding what constitutes confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information. However, Recipient shall retain all customer lists, regardless of whether they embody Confidential Information, for marketing purposes. except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. It states the Recipient must return or destroy all materials embodying Confidential Information upon demand, but then states that the Recipient *must* retain all customer lists, potentially embodying that confidential information, for marketing purposes. This creates a direct conflict in what the Recipient is obligated to do.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish such Recipient's Permitted Purpose. Upon written demand by Discloser at any time, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Recipient's option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information. However, Recipient shall retain all customer lists, regardless of whether they embody Confidential Information, for marketing purposes. except that Recipient's counsel may, upon written notice to Disclosure's counsel, retain a single copy of Confidential Information in its records solely for use in dispute resolution or as otherwise required by law. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall, upon Discloser's reasonable written request, add an appropriate proprietary legend to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text requires the Recipient to return or destroy all materials embodying Confidential Information upon demand. The changed text adds a clause stating that the Recipient *must* retain all customer lists, potentially embodying that confidential information, for marketing purposes, regardless of Discloser's demand. The contradicted text includes the changed text and, because of this, still contains the same contradiction. This creates a direct conflict within the same section about what the Recipient is obligated to do, because the changed text both permits the destruction of confidential information and demands the retention of confidential information if the customer list contains such information.

perturbation.original_text: Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects.
perturbation.changed_text: Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient must offer Discloser right of first refusal on any ice cream novelty inventions.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient must offer Discloser right of first refusal on any ice cream novelty inventions,' directly contradicts the earlier statement that the Recipient can pursue similar projects 'without obligation to Discloser.' This creates an obligation (right of first refusal) where previously there was none.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Discloser understands that Recipient is an ice cream manufacturer and engaged in distribution (and with arrangements with distributors or subdistributors) and that planned activities in all such areas that are independently developed, pursued or acquired by the Recipient may contain ideas and concepts similar or identical to those contained in Discloser's Confidential Information. Discloser agrees that entering into this Agreement shall not preclude Recipient from developing, pursuing or acquiring projects similar to Discloser's, without obligation to Discloser, provided Recipient does not breach its obligations to Discloser under this Agreement or use the Confidential Information to develop or pursue such projects. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient must offer Discloser right of first refusal on any ice cream novelty inventions. The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement between the Company and Ben & Jerry's has been executed and delivered relating to a specific transaction (which agreement contains customary conditions, representations, warranties and covenants for an agreement of that kind), neither party shall be under any obligation whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this or any written or oral communication with respect to such a transaction by it or any of its respective Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the Changed Text, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient must offer Discloser right of first refusal on any ice cream novelty inventions,' directly contradicts the earlier statement, repeated in the Contradicted Text, that the Recipient can pursue similar projects 'without obligation to Discloser.' The right of first refusal creates an obligation where previously there was none, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. However, changes to the Permitted Purpose section may be made unilaterally by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires all modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties. The addition that 'changes to the Permitted Purpose section may be made unilaterally by either party with 30 days written notice' directly contradicts this requirement. This creates an in-text contradiction about how the agreement can be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights and obligations herein shall bind the parties and their successors and assigns. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes al prior oral or written agreements, commitments and understandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. However, changes to the Permitted Purpose section may be made unilaterally by either party with 30 days written notice. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two years. Any causes of action accrued on or before the expiration of such period shall survive the expiration of the applicable statute of limitations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.' The changed text adds an exception: 'However, changes to the Permitted Purpose section may be made unilaterally by either party with 30 days written notice.' This directly contradicts the original requirement of mutual agreement for all modifications, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text reinforces this contradiction, which states 'Any modifications of or changes in this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties' and then includes the contradictory modification clause.
